svg.title: Three-schema architecture of a DBMS
svg.desc: Three levels of database abstraction: external (user views at the top), conceptual (logical schema in the middle), and internal (physical storage at the bottom), with bidirectional mapping arrows between each level.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a user application in the three-schema architecture?')
sendPrompt('How do different user apps access the same database differently?')
sendPrompt('What is an admin view in DBMS three-schema architecture?')
sendPrompt('What does the student external view contain in GreenGen?')
sendPrompt('What does the teacher external view contain in GreenGen?')
sendPrompt('What does the admin external view contain in GreenGen?')

svg.text: (community view — what data exists and how it is related)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What tables make up the GreenGen conceptual schema?')

svg.text: (how data is physically stored on disk — managed by MySQL)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the internal physical schema look like in MySQL for GreenGen?')
g.text: InnoDB tables · B-tree indexes · file segments · buffer pool

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is the GreenGen MySQL database physically stored?')
sendPrompt('How is the GreenGen MySQL database physically stored?')
